definition: Change In State of Delivery — a pre-CHoCH signal where price shifts from consistently delivering bearish candles to bullish ones (or vice versa). The LAST candle of the old delivery state becomes the entry zone.
depends_on: market_structure (CISD is a refinement of CHoCH detection)
displacement (CISD is confirmed when displacement follows the state change)
feeds_into: order_blocks (the CISD candle IS an order block)
entry_models (CISD provides earlier entry than waiting for full CHoCH)
related_to: power_of_three_and_AMD (CISD marks the transition from manipulation to distribution)
bridge_integration: CISD is detected on the M15 trigger TF and surfaces as a confluence factor, an advanced_bonus contributor, a synergy input, a swing-trade-type signal, and a prompt-injection trigger. (1) Detection: bridge/ict_pipeline.py:727-729 calls analysis.ict.core.detect_cisd(df_primary) when the primary df has >=10 bars, then get_latest_cisd(cisd_signals, direction=direction, max_age_bars=20) to filter for direction-matching CISDs within the last 20 M15 bars (~5h). result.has_cisd is True iff a fresh direction-aligned CISD exists. The card's candle-counting rules (3+ same-direction body candles, reversal close beyond the body extreme, body-not-wick) are enforced by the upstream detector, NOT re-implemented in the bridge. (2) Advanced factor: ict_pipeline.py:1123-1124 inserts the literal 'CISD' at the front of advanced_factors when has_cisd is True. This contributes +2.5 toward the per-cycle advanced bonus (capped at +10 across all factors, ict_pipeline.py:1128-1130). (3) Synergy: synergy_scorer.py:107-111 _has_cisd reads has_cisd OR any confluence_factor containing 'cisd' (case-insensitive); the 'CISD + PO3 phase transition' synergy at synergy_scorer.py:370-375 awards +5 when _has_cisd AND _in_distribution_phase (NY or overlap session). The named tag is 'CISD+PO3'. There is no separate CISD-only synergy and no CISD-vs-CHoCH synergy — the +5 fires only when paired with NY/overlap distribution. (4) Trade-type classification: claude_decision.py:351-358 classify_trade_type counts has_cisd as one of the 5 'swing' signals (alongside displacement_confirmed, ob_score>=12, structure_score>=20, sweep_detected); >=2 signals AND htf_aligned promotes the trade to 'swing' type, which affects position management (longer hold, wider TP). (5) Prompt injection: concept_injector.py:110-111 adds 'CISD' to the triggered concept set when has_cisd is True; concept_injector.py:239-241 also includes a fallback short-form pick 'Candle-level phase change confirmed — earliest reversal signal' when adv_factors contains 'cisd'. The full CISD card is eligible for the up-to-8-concept prompt block. (6) NOT a hard gate. The card's 'HTF alignment is mandatory for CISD trades' rule (because CISD has lower structural confirmation) is delivered via prompt context only — there is no pre-gate or post-gate that blocks counter-HTF CISD entries. The bridge does have an HTF Alignment Gate (claude_decision.py pre-gate #5) that already blocks counter-HTF non-scalp trades for ALL setups, so the CISD-specific HTF requirement is implicitly enforced by that broader gate, not by CISD-aware code. (7) Order block linkage: the card's 'CISD candle IS an order block' claim is NOT structurally wired — has_cisd and ob_score live as independent fields. The bridge does not promote a CISD candle into the OB list. They co-fire when the upstream detect_order_blocks happens to identify the same candle as both, but no code path makes this explicit. (8) Position-sizing reduction: the card's CISD_vs_CHoCH section recommends 'enter with reduced size when only CISD fires (no CHoCH yet)'. This is NOT enforced — sizing is computed from ATR-based SL distance regardless of which structural signal fired. If CISD-aware sizing reduction is ever desired, it would belong in risk_bridge / live_executor_adapter sizing logic.
vs_CHoCH: CHoCH requires a swing point to be broken. CISD fires BEFORE that — it detects the shift in candle delivery. CISD is the early warning; CHoCH is the confirmation.

operational_trigger.definition: CISD fires when all three conditions are met simultaneously:
operational_trigger.condition_1: 3 or more consecutive candles deliver in ONE direction — all bearish bodies (bodies, not wicks) OR all bullish bodies. Wicks on the opposite side are acceptable; BODIES must be same-direction.
operational_trigger.condition_2: The NEXT candle's body closes BEYOND the prior candle's body extreme in the opposite direction. For bullish CISD: body closes ABOVE the body high of the last bearish candle. For bearish CISD: body closes BELOW the body low of the last bullish candle.
operational_trigger.condition_3: That reversal candle IS the entry zone — its body defines the order block. The 50% of the reversal candle body is the optimal entry. The full body is the entry zone range.

candle_counting_rules.minimum: 3 consecutive same-direction BODY candles required before CISD can trigger. Fewer than 3 = not a valid CISD setup.
candle_counting_rules.optimal: 4-6 consecutive candles of same delivery produce the highest probability CISD. More candles = more compressed delivery = more powerful reversal.
candle_counting_rules.the_trigger_candle: The 4th+ candle (the reversal candle) is the CISD candle. It becomes both the signal and the entry zone.
candle_counting_rules.invalidation: If ANY candle in the 3+ sequence has a body that closes beyond the delivery extreme (e.g., a bullish candle body closes above a bearish sequence extreme), restart the count.

CISD_vs_CHoCH_timing.CISD_timing: CISD fires 1-3 candles BEFORE CHoCH confirms. It is the early entry signal.
CISD_vs_CHoCH_timing.CHoCH_timing: CHoCH requires a swing point break — this takes at least 1-2 additional candles beyond CISD.
CISD_vs_CHoCH_timing.trade_off: CISD = earlier entry, better R:R, lower structural confirmation. CHoCH = later entry, worse R:R, higher structural confirmation.
CISD_vs_CHoCH_timing.recommendation: Use CISD only when HTF alignment is already confirmed. The reduced confirmation is acceptable when HTF context is strong.

risk_reward.CISD_entry: Entry at 50% of CISD candle body (OTE within CISD). SL below CISD candle low (bullish) or above CISD candle high (bearish).
risk_reward.advantage: Earlier entry = smaller SL distance = larger position size for same risk = better R:R. Target remains same HTF draw.
risk_reward.disadvantage: No structural confirmation yet. Price could continue the original delivery direction. This is why HTF alignment is mandatory.
risk_reward.typical_RR: CISD entries often achieve 5:1 to 10:1 R:R when HTF aligned, vs 2:1 to 4:1 for waiting for CHoCH confirmation.

timeframe_application.high_conviction: M15 CISD inside an H4 order block with H4 bullish bias = maximum conviction entry.
timeframe_application.medium_conviction: M15 CISD at H4 equilibrium (50% of H4 dealing range) with daily bias aligned.
timeframe_application.low_conviction: M15 CISD against H4 trend direction = do not trade. The CISD is likely just a pullback within the H4 delivery.
timeframe_application.rule: CISD timeframe must be AT LEAST 2 degrees lower than the HTF bias timeframe (H4 bias requires M15 or lower CISD; Daily bias requires H1 or lower CISD).

entry_mechanics.step_1: Identify the CISD candle — the reversal candle that closes against the prior 3+ candle delivery direction.
entry_mechanics.step_2: Mark the CISD candle body: top of body (high) and bottom of body (low). This zone is the order block.
entry_mechanics.step_3: Wait for price to retrace BACK INTO the CISD candle body. Do not chase the initial move.
entry_mechanics.step_4: Optimal entry is at the 50% level of the CISD candle body (the midpoint).
entry_mechanics.step_5_bullish: For bullish CISD: place SL below the CISD candle low (below the wick, not just the body).
entry_mechanics.step_5_bearish: For bearish CISD: place SL above the CISD candle high.
entry_mechanics.TP: First target = prior swing high (for bullish) or prior swing low (for bearish). Extended target = HTF draw on liquidity.

PO3_connection: CISD typically occurs at the exact moment PO3 transitions from manipulation to distribution. It IS the phase transition signal.
how_to_trade: When candle delivery shifts (3+ bearish candles followed by 1 bullish candle with body close above prior candle high), the bullish candle's low is the entry zone. Enter on retest. SL below the manipulation extreme.
